object_text: Son of Mostafa Qoli Khan E'temad al-Saltaneh; grandson of Mohammad Baqer Khan; great-grandson of Haji Mohammad Khan Jeyhunabadi.
Mohtaj Ali Khan was a son of Abdollah Khan.
Mansur Ali Khan was a son of Abdollah Khan.
Held the titles Ejlal al-Mamalek and Amir Arfa'.
Held the title Sardar Akram.
Letgah was the principal center of the Amir Nezam Letgahi family.
Kabudarahang was one of the family's important landed areas.
Gholamhossein Khan descends from Mohtaj Ali Khan and is not a child of Gholam Ali.
The book's figure shows no descendants for Mansur Ali Khan.
Hossein Qoli Khan was a son of Abdollah Khan.
He was described as Ejlal al-Dowleh and Amir Tuman.
He received Sa'ed al-Saltaneh at his father's request.
Mohtaj Ali Khan received Ejlal al-Mamalek at his father's request.
Abdollah Khan was appointed governor of Khuzestan and titled Sardar Akram.
S0001 explicitly indexes Mohtaj Ali Khan on printed pages 61 and 152.
Nabi Khan was a son of Mohammad Hossein Khan Gharagozloo.
Nabi Khan commanded thousands of Gharagozloo troops in Mohammad Shah’s Herat campaign.
Abdollah Khan, holding the rank of yavar, supported Nabi Khan’s forces in the Herat campaign.
notes: Kept separate from P0004 pending identity resolution.

object_text: After being wounded at Herat, Abdollah Khan received the rank of sarhang.
Shir Ali Khan repeatedly climbed the fortress wall and attempted to plant the victory flag.
Shir Ali Khan received a robe of honor and decoration for bravery.
Rashid Khan, a colonel of the Mokhberan regiment, was wounded in the leg during the Herat fighting.
Nabi Khan died shortly after being removed from the battlefield with severe wounds.
Nabi Khan was transferred with other Gharagozloo dead for burial in Mashhad by order of Mohammad Shah.
Mahmoud Khan was the son of Nabi Khan and a descendant of Mohammad Hossein Khan Ashiqloo.
The Gharagozloo guard defending Bushehr numbered fifty soldiers.
British forces dispersed the small Gharagozloo guard and occupied Bushehr to pressure Iran over Herat.
Gharagozloo troops helped assault the Herat fortifications and open the fortress gates.
